meta.title: LifeCareLog — Care for your life, one service at a time
meta.description: A one-person dev studio building small, meaningful services for everyday life.

hero.tagline: Designed to be simple — small changes that matter
hero.headline:
Building services
life actually needs.
hero.subheadline:
Solo developer · AI product builder.
Running a finance calculator, legal info tool, and basketball club app.
hero.brandPill: Care for your life, in every log

philosophy.headline:
I build services
for anyone
who needs them.
philosophy.description:
Investment platforms, mobility apps,
blockchain accounting, edtech.
Five years of building across different domains taught me something.

What seems trivial to one person
can change someone else's entire day.

So if even a few people need it, I'll build it.
Small tools that care for the parts of life we tend to overlook.
That's why I started LifeCareLog.
philosophy.quote:
My goal is to contribute by building services
that genuinely improve people's lives.

story.chapter1Title: Five years as a frontend developer
story.chapter1:
Fractional investment platforms, vehicle search apps,
blockchain accounting, edtech content editors.

Four startups over five years.
More than ten products — all on the frontend side.

I always loved building products,
but my role was always limited to the frontend.
Building something entirely on my own felt out of reach.
story.chapter2:
One day, I was let go without much warning.

It was jarring at first — but looking back,
it gave me space to sit with a question I'd been avoiding.

"I've only ever done frontend.
Could I actually build something from start to finish on my own?"
story.chapter3:
As AI tools got better,
I thought maybe I could give it a shot.

Planning, design, backend, deployment —
everything was new. I wrestled with countless errors,
learning as I went.

It's slow and clumsy,
but I'm building services that at least one person can use,
with my own hands.
story.chapter4:
I needed a loan calculator — and got ads instead.
I tried reading a contract — and hit a wall of legal jargon.
On a tough day, there was nowhere to quietly write things down.

Starting from frustrations I've actually lived,
I'm building the things life needs — one at a time.

services.headline: What I'm building
services.subheadline: From mortgage math to housing-law research, basketball club ops, and journaling your feelings — I build services around problems I've actually had to solve.
services.vision: Four services now, but someday A to Z — I want to build tools that care for every part of life.

planC.title: Plan-C
planC.subtitle: Financial Calculators & Planner
planC.description:
I built Plan-C for my own home-loan and moving-budget calculations.
It keeps common calculations like loan interest, severance pay, and take-home salary clean and fast, with scenario comparison and planner tools built in.
planC.features: Loan interest · Severance pay · Take-home salary · Savings calculator · Scenario planner

planL.title: Plan-L
planL.subtitle: Statute & Precedent Search
planL.description:
I built Plan-L while looking up housing lease protection laws and court precedents for a real lease dispute.
It is not legal advice — it is an information search tool for finding relevant statutes and precedents in plain language.
planL.teaser: AI searches statutes and precedents, then summarizes the results in readable language.
planL.features: Precedent search · Statute search · Plain-language summaries · AI-assisted information organization

planT.title: Plan-T
planT.subtitle: AI Mental Wellness App
planT.description:
How are you feeling today? Why?
When it's hard to put into words, AI listens quietly.
Plan-T is an iOS/Android app for daily mental wellness — in active development.
planT.features: Mood tracking · AI conversation · Wellness reports · Daily check-in

planB.title: Plan-B
planB.subtitle: Basketball Club Ops Manager
planB.description:
I built Plan-B for my own amateur basketball club — to keep games, members, dues, and tactics in one place.
Log quarter-by-quarter stats, organize team rosters, track dues, and plan plays from a clean mobile-first interface.
planB.features: Game scoring · Members & teams · Dues tracking · Tactics board · Season stats

contact.headline: What's on your mind?
contact.subheadline:

I read every message and reply personally.

about.headline:
Building services
that care for life.
about.description: I build and run everything solo, with AI. Whenever I find something inconvenient, I make a tool to fix it. Complex financial calculations, confusing legal terms, the hassle of running a team — solving them one by one.
about.skills: Next.js · React · Expo · FastAPI · Supabase · AI

how.headline: How I build.
how.principle1Body: Launch an MVP quickly, watch how people use it, and improve from there.
how.principle2Body: Development, design, planning — AI is involved at every step so things get done faster and more carefully.
how.principle3Body: Launch isn't the end. I'm responsible until the product runs stably.
how.pricingLink: See pricing and full service details

meta.title: Plan-T — An AI chat journal for your feelings
meta.description: Plan-T lets you write your day as a chat. The AI quietly listens and distills your scattered feelings into a single line. Join the waitlist.

brand.name: Plan-T
brand.tagline: An AI chat journal for your feelings

hero.headline:
Just let today's
feelings out.
hero.subheadline:
Plan-T is a journaling app where you write your day as a chat —
the AI listens quietly and distills your feelings into one line.
hero.note: No spam — just one note when we launch.

pain.headline:
Your mind is full,
but the page stays blank.
pain.item1Body: You open the journal, can't decide where to start, and just close it again.
pain.item2Body: You feel off but can't quite tell yourself why, or what set it off.
pain.item3Body: Bringing every small feeling to someone else feels like too much to ask.

demo.headline:
One question, one line —
you write like a conversation.
demo.caption: A static demo screen example. The final product design may differ at launch.
demo.msg1: What feeling was strongest for you today?
demo.msg2: Meetings kept piling up, so I'm pretty drained.
demo.msg3: A heavy day. Even so, you carried it through to the end — that's real.
demo.msg4: Hearing that actually eases it a little.
demo.summary: Overwhelming, but you held on to the end.

features.headline:
Beyond recording —
a gentle flow that cares.
features.card1Body: So you never freeze at a blank screen, Plan-T asks how your day was first.
features.card2Body: It listens to the scatter and reflects your core feeling back in one soft sentence.
features.card3Body: From your records, look back calmly on where your heart has been lately.
features.card4Body: As your day winds down, one gentle check-in helps the habit stick.

safety.headline:
Talking about feelings
should be safe.
safety.point1Body: Plan-T is a journaling tool that helps you sort your feelings. It does not replace medical diagnosis or treatment.
safety.point2Body: At launch, record protection and deletion controls will be designed clearly. This landing v0 does not store journal records.
safety.point3Body: For urgent or high-risk situations, we are preparing clear guidance to professional support channels.
safety.disclaimer: In a crisis, please contact emergency services or a local crisis line right away.

cta.headline:
Want to be the first
to write it down?
cta.body: We'll let you know the moment we launch. Just leave your email.
cta.success: Email format looks valid. Actual waitlist storage will be connected only after deployment approval.
cta.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again shortly.
cta.privacy: This v0 form is a local demo and does not store your email. Before real collection, privacy policy and consent copy will be connected.

planT.footerNote: Plan-T is a journaling service made by LifeCareLog.
